page: Microsoft Azure FX12ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine instance belonging to the FXmsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 12 vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. The instance is equipped with 252.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX12ms_v2 demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2349.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 34.88 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Text processing performance is also in the top tier with a Geekbench score of 3015.0. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11866.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 13337.6. Memory bandwidth reaches 1037.5 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 94.15 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 339.89 tokens/sec using a small 135M parameter model, though performance scales down to 12.33 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model.

The hardware profile of the FX12ms_v2 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that require substantial RAM per core without the overhead of high vCPU counts. However, the lack of local storage and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external premium storage attachment for data-intensive operations and limiting its utility for heavy parallel GPU computing. Given the high memory capacity, dedicated CPU allocation, and strong single-threaded performance, this instance is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise applications, software compilation, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure FX12ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It provides 12 vCPUs across 6 physical cores, backed by a substantial 252.0 GB of RAM, translating to 21.0 GB of memory per core. While the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it is premium storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2349.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.88 GB/sec, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. This resource profile delivers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not require massive parallel core counts. It is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise applications, software compilation, and lightweight LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX12ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 12 vCPUs, 252 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor at 4.2 GHz, optimized for memory-heavy enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-core density and strong single-threaded performance for demanding enterprise database workloads.
